DECLARATION OF ROBERT MANCHUK IN SUPPORT OF MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST FOR DECEASED PLAINTIFF AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT (TRANSACTION ID # 63155095) FILED BY PLAINTIFF WITIUK, VERA V

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION - PLAINTIFF VERA WITIUK'S MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST FOR DECEASED PLAINTIFF AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO MAY 21, 2019. BY NO LATER THAN MAY 14, 2019 MOVING PARTY TO PROVIDE A SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEF AND DECLARATION ADDRESSING THE FOLLOWING: (1) BASED ON THE SIZE OF THE ESTATE DOES DECEDENT'S WILL HAVE TO BE PROBATED; (2) IF SO, UNDER WHAT LAW THE WILL SHALL BE PROBATED, I.E. WHERE WAS THE DECEDENT DOMICILED AT THE TIME OF HER DEATH FOR THE PURPOSES OF PROBATE; (3) WHETHER DECEDENT'S ESTATE HAD BEEN/IS BEING ADMINISTERED WITHIN OR WITHOUT CALIFORNIA. ADDITIONALLY, MOVING PARTIES TO PROVIDE A COPY OF THE WILL FOR IN CAMERA REVIEW. OPPOSING PARTY MAY SUBMIT A SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION NO LATER THAN MAY 17, 2019. JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES; CLERK: MARIA OLOPERNES; NOT REPORTED; D501.

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, MOTION - PLAINTIFF VERA WITIUK'S MOTION TO SUBSTITUTE SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST FOR DECEASED PLAINTIFF AND FOR LEAVE TO FILE A SECOND AMENDED COMPLAINT IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. MOVING PARTY FAILED TO PROPERLY AND ADEQUATELY ADDRESS (EVEN AFTER BEING GIVEN AN OPPORTUNITY TO PROVIDE SUPPLEMENTAL BRIEFING) ITS ENTITLEMENT TO BE SUBSTITUTED IN. A SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST IS THE PROPER SUBSTITUTE PLAINTIFF ONLY IF THERE IS NO PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE WHO HAS A SUPERIOR RIGHT TO BE SUBSTITUTED FOR THE DECEDENT. CCP 377.31, CCP 377.32. NO AUTHORITY IS PROVIDED AND NO ARGUMENT IS MADE AS TO PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE'S IN CANADIAN PROBATE ACTION RIGHTS AND OBLIGATIONS IN REGARDS TO THIS ACTION. THE COURT ALSO NOTES THAT NO NOTICE OF THIS MOTION WAS PROVIDED TO THE PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE. AS THIS MOTION IS DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE, MOVING PARTY MAY FILE ANOTHER MOTION IF AND WHEN APPROPRIATE IN LIGHT OF THIS RULING. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER IN COMPLIANCE WITH CRC3.1312(B). JUDGE CHARLES F. HAINES, CLERK: N. WONG, NOT REPORTED (D501)

MASTER CALENDAR JURY
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Continued To Nov-12-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Personal Representative Or Alternatively, Successors In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar Jul-01-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Michael Pietrykowski, Esq. of Gordon & Rees LLP, 415-986-5900, appearing for Vera Witiuk, Plaintiff and moving party.

Allysin Murphy, Esq. (No card provided)

ARGUED. Ms. Murphy requested that the hearing be advanced from the court's tentative ruling and provided a briefing schedule. Mr. Pietrykowski reported that he had no objections to this request. The new schedule is as follows:

The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:
Hearing on motion to substitute personal representative: August 2, 2019
Moving papers filed by: July 12, 2019
Opposition papers due: July 22, 2019
Reply due by: July 26, 2019

Parties waive notice. Judge: Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not Reported. (D501)

MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Successor In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-21-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearances.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF VERA WITIUK's Motion To Substitute Successor In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to properly and adequately address (even after being given an opportunity to provide supplemental briefing) its entitlement to be substituted in. A successor in interest is the proper substitute plaintiff only if there is no personal representative who has a superior right to be substituted for the decedent. CCP 377.31, CCP 377.32. No authority is provided and no argument is made as to Personal Representative's in Canadian probate action rights and obligations in regards to this action. The Court also notes that no notice of this motion was provided to the Personal Representative. As this motion is denied without prejudice, moving party may file another motion if and when appropriate in light of this ruling. Prevailing party to prepare order in compliance with CRC3.1312(b). Judge Charles F. Haines, Clerk: N. Wong, Not reported (D501)

MOTION - Notice Of Motion And Motion To Substitute Successor In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint
Off Calendar May-08-2019 Continued To May-21-19 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 501
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. Tentative ruling is adopted as follows: PLAINTIFF VERA WITIUK'S MOTION To Substitute Successor In Interest For Deceased Plaintiff And For Leave To File A Second Amended Complaint is CONTINUED to May 21, 2019. By no later than May 14, 2019 moving party to provide a supplemental brief and declaration addressing the following: (1) based on the size of the estate does decedent's will have to be probated; (2) if so, under what law the will shall be probated, i.e. where was the decedent domiciled at the time of her death for the purposes of probate; (3) whether decedent's estate had been/is being administered within or without California. Additionally, Moving parties to provide a copy of the will for in camera review. Opposing party may submit a supplemental opposition no later than May 17, 2019.
Judge Charles F. Haines; Clerk: Maria Olopernes; Not Reported; D501.
